title: Trump Faces the Complicated Reality of a Costly, Unpopular War in Iran
summary: President Trump’s predictions of a relatively short-term conflict with minimal economic consequences appear to be crumbling.

title: Jordanians Struggle as Mideast Wars Scare Tourists Away
summary: Though Jordan mostly sat out the conflicts, its tourism high season was nearly wiped out at popular sites. Visitors canceled flights, hotels and tours.

title: OPEC Plus, in Symbolic Gesture, to Increase Oil Production
summary: The announcement came days after the United Arab Emirates withdrew from the group. The higher output will have little effect on global supplies.

title: Blanche Says Others Who Post ‘86 47’ Message Won’t Be Charged Like Comey
summary: The acting attorney general said such messages were “posted constantly” and said the Justice Department had gathered other evidence against the former F.B.I. director, but declined to describe it.

title: New Video Analysis Suggests Suspect in Correspondents’ Dinner Attack Fired First
summary: A Times analysis of footage released by the F.B.I. on Thursday night appears to show the assailant shot at a Secret Service officer, who then returned fire.

title: In a Reversal, Doctors From Countries Under Trump’s Travel Ban Are Allowed to Stay in U.S.
summary: In a reversal, the Trump administration has exempted foreign physicians from a visa application freeze that was pushing many out of jobs in underserved areas.

title: Behind Supreme Court Voting Rights Ruling, a Clash Over the Reality of Racism
summary: The Supreme Court ruling said there must be proof that a racial group was “intentionally” disadvantaged. The dissent called it “well-nigh impossible.”

title: A California Dream? Some Democrats Fear Harris Picked the Wrong Race.
summary: Some wish Kamala Harris had decided to run for governor in California, where Democrats are struggling to break through, rather than weigh another White House run.

title: Indiana Primary Election Will Test Trump’s Influence With Republicans
summary: The election on Tuesday will test the influence of President Trump, who endorsed challengers to Republican state senators who scuttled his push to redraw political maps.

title: The Last Days of Butter Ridge
summary: The Watsons were dairy farmers for generations, the rhythms of their lives defined by their cows. Until this spring.

title: These Countries Embrace E.V.s to Avoid Oil Price Shocks
summary: People in Costa Rica and other Latin American, Asian and African countries are increasingly buying electric vehicles to avoid spiking fuel prices.

title: Driving Electric in Costa Rica Is Surprisingly Doable
summary: The charging network is spotty, but it’s a small country.

title: Electric Cars Were a Big Deal Over a Hundred Years Ago
summary: The very first E.V. was born in the 1830s. By the 1900s, electric carriages were dodging horse droppings in Manhattan streets.

title: Khloé Kardashian Is a Music All Day Kind of Person
summary: “I’m big on a soundtrack to my life,” the TV star and podcast host said. Hers includes Sabrina Carpenter, Frank Sinatra and John Coltrane.

title: Why the A.I. Job Apocalypse (Probably) Won’t Happen
summary: The feeling of efficiency should be mistrusted.

title: How Lauren Sánchez Bezos Inspired ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’
summary: The real-life Met Gala vs. the one onscreen.

title: Bruce vs. Donald
summary: We look at Bruce Springsteen’s political activism.

title: Spirit Airlines’ Demise Could Help Other Airlines
summary: Even in its reduced state, the company played an important role in forcing other airlines to keep fares low, some experts said.

title: 2 U.S. Service Members Are Missing After Military Exercise in Morocco
summary: The two Army soldiers were reported missing during a joint military exercise on Saturday near Cap Draa Training Area, a coastal area in southwestern Morocco.

title: Norway’s Northern Lights Nightmare
summary: This Arctic region has seen a boom in tourism, but as tourists chase the northern lights, scammers chase their money.

title: ‘Devil Wears Prada’ Sequel Leads Box Office With $77 Million
summary: It was the biggest opening weekend for a traditional comedy in 11 years. One analyst called the debut “sensational.”
